name: Mottoes
strauss_status: EXPLICITLY NAMED — EXAMPLES REQUIRE CONTINUED COLLECTION

source.primary_work: Persecution and the Art of Writing
source.primary_essay: The Literary Character of the Guide for the Perplexed
source.source_formulation: "mottoes prefixed to the whole work or to individual parts"
source.source_mechanism: A prefatory saying or scriptural phrase supplies an indirect interpretive key to the work or section it precedes.
source.source_documentary_function: The motto communicates through allusion rather than direct exposition.
source.documentary_limit: The present source names the device, but examples and internal varieties require continued documentary collection.

mechanism: A brief text placed before an entire work or a bounded internal division stands outside the ordinary exposition yet bears a recoverable relation to what follows. Through quotation, allusion, compression, contrast, or source association, it may orient the reader toward a problem, distinction, sequence, or interpretive horizon that the following unit does not state directly.
investigative_requirement: Establish that the prefixed text belongs to the authorially relevant witness and is not an editorial addition; recover its source and original context where possible; define exactly which work or part it governs; reconstruct the ordinary surface function before proposing an allusive relation; map the motto's words, source context, and placement to specific features of the governed unit; and test decorative, thematic, devotional, generic, publisher-supplied, and conventional alternatives.
documentary_constraints: LC-013 requires prefatory placement before a whole work or individual part. A quotation embedded within ordinary exposition is not a motto merely because it is brief.
The motto's source text and original context must be preserved separately from the author's use of it.
A motto may offer an interpretive key, but thematic resemblance or decoration alone does not establish a concealment-related function.
The governed scope must be explicit: whole work, book, part, chapter, or other recoverable division.
The allusive relation may involve correspondence, contrast, qualification, sequence, or source association; it must not be reduced automatically to simple thematic summary.
The evaluator may corroborate a motto-to-scope relation but may not infer hidden teaching, authorial intention, intended audience, or the true interpretation of the governed unit.

trigger_conditions: A brief prefixed text is recoverable in a stable witness.
The text precedes and is documentarily attached to a whole work or a bounded internal part.
Its quotation, allusion, or source relation is recoverable or explicitly preserved as unresolved.
At least one specific documentary relation connects the motto to the governed unit.
The proposed relation exceeds mere decoration, attribution, or generic thematic resemblance.
minimum_evidence: Exact motto text and stable witness location.
Evidence that the motto is present in the authorially relevant witness.
Evidence excluding or identifying publisher, editor, translator, or later-hand addition.
Exact governed scope and its textual boundary.
Source attribution for the motto where recoverable.
Original source passage and source context where recoverable.
Translation and source-language form where relevant.
A candidate allusive-relation record tied to exact words or source features.
Local and architectonic relation between motto and governed unit.
Comparison with other mottoes in the work or authorized corpus when a pattern is proposed.
An auditable evidence path from witness placement and source recovery to the allusive-relation claim.
corroboration_indicators: The governed unit repeats distinctive words or images from the motto.
The unit's structure follows a sequence implied by the motto or its source context.
A contradiction or difficulty in the unit is clarified by the motto's source context.
The author elsewhere cites or explains the same source in a compatible way.
Multiple mottoes in the work display a systematic relation to their governed parts.
The motto appears at an architectonically marked boundary.
A source comparison shows deliberate selection, truncation, alteration, or repositioning of the quoted text.
Another independently reconstructed literary device converges on the same bounded interpretive problem.
ordinary_alternatives: Publisher, editor, translator, or later-hand addition.
A source acknowledgment without interpretive force.
A memorable phrase chosen for style.
A broad thematic resemblance produced by common vocabulary.
The investigator has overextended the motto's governed scope.
disqualifying_conditions: The prefixed text is absent from the authorially relevant witness.
The text was supplied only by an editor, publisher, translator, or later hand and is not authorially adopted.
The governed scope cannot be established.
The text is embedded within ordinary exposition rather than prefixed to a work or part.
No specific relation to the governed unit is documented.
The relation depends only on broad thematic similarity.
The proposed source attribution is false or unsupported.
The source context contradicts the proposed relation without a principled account.
Decoration, dedication, invocation, or genre convention fully explains the placement.
The analysis assumes an interpretive key before reconstructing the motto's ordinary function.
authorized_response: Preserve the motto, witness, placement, and governed scope.
Create separate source-text, source-context, surface-function, and allusive-relation records.
Record editorial or authorial status explicitly.
Map exact lexical, imagistic, structural, and source-context correspondences.
Search the governed unit for repetitions, contrasts, sequences, and counterexamples.
Compare all other mottoes within the declared scope.
Test decorative, thematic, devotional, generic, editorial, translational, and attribution alternatives.
Evaluate LC-015, LC-016, LC-017, LC-020, or LC-021 separately when independently triggered.
Preserve intended meaning and concealed instruction as unresolved.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer an interpretive key from prefatory placement alone.
Do not treat every epigraph or prefatory quotation as concealment.
Do not ignore editorial or publisher provenance.
Do not detach the motto from its original source context.
Do not extend the motto's scope beyond the documented division.
Do not infer hidden teaching, authorial intention, intended audience, or doctrinal truth.
Do not assume that the motto simply summarizes what follows.
Do not collapse LC-013 into citation, repetition, chapter heading, or hint without separate evaluation.
operational.uncertainty_rule: When a prefixed text is authentic and its governed scope is known but its source, ordinary function, or allusive relation remains contestable, preserve a candidate motto relation with competing source and scope explanations. Do not designate a hidden interpretive key.
operational.termination_rule: Terminate as NOT_TRIGGERED when no authentic prefixed text with a specific documented relation to a governed unit is present; as BLOCKED_MISSING_EVIDENCE when witness provenance, governed scope, source, context, function, comparison, or evidence path is incomplete; as candidate when ordinary alternatives or corroboration remain unresolved; and as corroborated only when the bounded motto-to-scope relation survives full documentary testing without establishing hidden teaching, intention, audience, or doctrinal truth.

version_history.change_note: Initial executable projection of LC-013 with witness-provenance, governed-scope, source-context, surface-function, allusive-relation, comparison, ordinary-alternative, and anti-overreading controls.
